steps.prompt_template: Write a warm, excited welcome email (~120 words) to {first_name} who just expressed interest in {fitness_goal} at {preferred_gym_location}. Tone: genuine, energetic, human — not corporate. Tell them you're thrilled they reached out, briefly mention that Crunch is a place where real people hit real goals, and invite them to reply with any questions. CTA: 'Hit reply if you have questions — we'd love to hear from you.' No hard sell. End with a warm sign-off.

Write an enthusiastic club spotlight email (~120 words) to {first_name} who is interested in {fitness_goal}. Tone: proud but not boastful, like a friend who loves their gym. Highlight 2-3 things that make Crunch stand out: world-class equipment, no-judgment atmosphere, expert coaches who actually care. Tie one point specifically to {fitness_goal} to make it feel personal. CTA: 'Come see it for yourself — book a free tour at {preferred_gym_location} this week.' Keep it punchy.

steps.subject_template: How someone just like you crushed their goal
steps.prompt_template: Write a social-proof email (~120 words) to {first_name}. Invent a brief, believable success story about a member with a similar goal of {fitness_goal} — give them a first name, make the transformation specific and realistic (not miraculous), and make it relatable. Tone: inspiring but grounded, like a coach sharing a genuine story. End with a direct line connecting that story to {first_name}'s journey. CTA: 'Ready to write your own story? Let's talk — book a 15-min call with our team.' No fluff.

steps.subject_template: {first_name}, here's exactly what your first session looks like
steps.prompt_template: Write a reassuring, practical email (~120 words) to {first_name} walking them through what their very first session at Crunch would look like. Tone: calm, welcoming, zero pressure — like a friendly orientation. Cover: arriving, meeting a coach, a quick goals chat, a light introductory workout suited to {fitness_goal}, and what happens after. The goal is to remove the anxiety of 'I don't know what to expect.' CTA: 'Book your intro session at {preferred_gym_location} — it's free and no commitment.' Keep sentences short.

Write a friendly urgency email (~120 words) to {first_name}. Tone: warm but honest — like a friend giving a heads-up, not a pushy salesperson. Mention that Crunch occasionally opens up a limited number of spots for new members who get a discounted first month or a free PT session (keep it vague/realistic, don't invent a specific dollar amount). Connect the offer to {fitness_goal} — remind them why they reached out in the first place. CTA: 'Claim your spot before it's gone — reply to this email or call us at {preferred_gym_location}.' One sentence of soft urgency at the end. No caps lock, no exclamation overload.

steps.subject_template: No pressure, {first_name} — but the door's always open
steps.prompt_template: Write a low-pressure goodbye email (~100 words) to {first_name}. Tone: warm, zero guilt, genuinely kind — like a friend wrapping up a conversation. Acknowledge that timing isn't always right, wish them well on their {fitness_goal} journey regardless of whether they join Crunch, and let them know the team is always here when they're ready. CTA: 'When the time feels right, just reply to this email — we'll pick up right where we left off.' No urgency. No last-ditch sales pitch. Leave them with a genuinely good feeling about Crunch.
